body_md:
## In a nutshell

**Market size is not in the record** (no TAM/SAM/SOM or spend-base cited). The structural problem Ferrite targets is LLM-agent CI regenerating overlapping build work across a fleet, so spend scales with redundant agent steps rather than unique artifacts [C1]. That load is urgent as agent-driven pipelines proliferate: yesterday’s remote cache was built for human-authored, relatively stable graphs; agent runs re-emit steps that a content-addressed cache can collapse [C2]. Ferrite’s product is a content-addressed build cache that deduplicates those agent-generated steps to cut CI cost [C1].

## Company

**Ferrite** — solo technical founder Mara Voss (Berlin, DE); first-time founder, no prior fundraise claimed [C3]. Raising a $500k pre-seed; applying for Example Fund I’s $100k fast check [C4]. Sector fit (ai-infrastructure / devtools) is implied by the product, not independently sized here.

## Traction snapshot (contested vs verified)

Deck claims **3,100 active installations across 240 organizations** and **$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months** [C5][C6]. Public registry shows **2,900 lifetime downloads** and **~340/week** — lifetime downloads sit *below* the claimed active-install count [C7]. That is an open contradiction, not a reconciled distribution story. Design partners (Series-B devtools; AI lab) and the **10x** agent-CI cost-reduction claim remain subject-only [C8][C9].

Independently archived product presence (not a substitute for cleared scale): GitHub — 412 stars, weekly releases for 20 weeks, founder-dominated commits [C10]; Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win (~400 teams) [C11]; June 2026 launch thread with three self-identified production users [C12]. Forum adjudication (agent-derived) treated deck install/MRR figures as uncleared non-evidence until reconciled, while keeping diligence open on shipping/launch presence rather than killing on registry totals alone [C13].

claims.text: Ferrite is a content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.

evidence.quote: Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.

claims.text: The product’s why-now is framed as agent-driven CI regenerating build steps across fleets, where content-addressed dedupe is newly valuable versus prior remote-cache assumptions.

evidence.quote: Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.
Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.

claims.text: Mara Voss is a solo technical founder based in Berlin; first-time founder with no prior fundraise claimed.

evidence.quote: Ex-infra at a mid-size fintech (4 years), wrote the internal remote-cache that cut CI spend 60%. First-time founder. No prior fundraise.

claims.text: Ferrite is raising a $500k pre-seed and applying for the $100k fast check.

evidence.quote: Raising a $500k pre-seed; applying for the $100K fast check.

claims.text: Deck asserts 3,100 active installations across 240 organizations.

evidence.quote: Deck page 7: '3,100 active installations across 240 organizations'.

claims.text: Deck asserts $18k MRR growing 40% month-over-month for 3 months.

evidence.quote: Deck page 9: '$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months'. No external corroboration attached.

claims.text: Public package registry shows ~340 downloads/week and 2,900 lifetime downloads — below the deck’s claimed active-install count.

evidence.quote: Public package registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime. Tension with deck's '3,100 active installations' — lifetime downloads are below the claimed active-install count.

claims.text: Two design partners claimed: one Series-B devtools company and one AI lab (names withheld).

evidence.quote: Two design partners: one Series-B devtools company, one AI lab (names withheld in deck)

claims.text: Deck/application claim 10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads (internal benchmark).

evidence.quote: 10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads (internal benchmark)
Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.

claims.text: GitHub repo shows sustained shipping: 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors, ~1,400 commits over 11 months (86% founder), weekly releases for 20 weeks.

evidence.quote: Repository: 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors. 1,400 commits over 11 months, 86% by the founder. Weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks. CI config shows real test coverage.

claims.text: Ferrite won the infrastructure track at Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 in a field of ~400 teams.

evidence.quote: Winner, infrastructure track, Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026 — 'Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI'. Judged field of ~400 teams.

claims.text: June 2026 launch post reached a major aggregator front page (~6 hours, 214 points, 89 comments); three commenters identified as production users.

evidence.quote: Launch post, 2nd June: front page of a major aggregator for ~6 hours, 214 points, 89 comments. Founder answered 41 comments in-thread with technical depth; three commenters identified themselves as production users.

claims.text: Forum adjudication treated deck 3,100 installs / 240 orgs and $18k MRR / +40% MoM as uncleared non-evidence, while keeping Ferrite in diligence on verified shipping and launch presence rather than killing solely on public registry totals.

evidence.quote: Deck page 7: '3,100 active installations across 240 organizations'. Deck page 9: '$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months'. No external corroboration attached.
Public package registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime. Tension with deck's '3,100 active installations' — lifetime downloads are below the claimed active-install count.

gaps.note: No TAM/SAM/SOM, category spend, or agent-CI cost base in application, deck signals, or independent archives — nutshell cannot state sizing beyond ‘not disclosed’.
No competitor cluster, substitute remote-cache vendors, or share estimates on file.
$18k MRR and +40% MoM are deck-only; no payment-processor or billing export on file.
No product telemetry/admin export defining ‘active install’ or org count for the deck window; distribution-channel map (registry vs private mirrors/binaries/containers/self-host) absent.
Partner names withheld; no written confirmation or intro on file.
Internal benchmark claimed; protocol, workload mix, and customer before/after CI spend artifacts not on file.
Ex-fintech infra / ~60% CI cut from internal remote-cache is subject-claimed only; no manager/peer reference on file.
Launch-thread self-identified users not contacted; no structured customer references with workload/spend context.
Entity, jurisdiction, and IP assignment not disclosed in the record used for this snapshot.
